what_it_does: A Claude-Design-ready brief for the Company's cognition AUTHORING UI — the operator screen for creating/editing the roles and rules of the concurrent-cognition system. It states what the screen is, the proposed information architecture (role authoring, rule authoring, preview-a-turn, approval loop), the exact backend contract (endpoints with file:line citations and response shapes), the propose->approve->apply UX flow, the design bar, nine hard laws, and open questions for Tim.
observations: Explicitly addressed to Claude Design and grounded in the real repo with file:line citations for verifiability.
No-fiction note: the backend is built/tested/live but NO authoring front-end exists; the only living cognition surface is the read-only CognitionView.tsx (the VIEW).

Explains the cognition model: an utterance fires roles in parallel emitting structured output to run://<turn>/<role>, rules read resolved values and route to one of five destinations, the reply assembles in staged parts.
Proposes one Authoring region (sibling of canvas regions) with role/rule/preview panels coexisting beside CognitionView, reusing NodeConfigForm/Inspector/Inbox/ProposeAffordance patterns.

Corrects an endpoint-count error from the originating task: there is no /api/cognition/role/apply; apply rides the existing operator-only /api/apply. Counts 12 cognition authoring endpoints (9 POST + 3 GET) plus 2 reads plus 2 shared operator endpoints.

Tabulates the full backend contract: READ (cognition_info, SSE stream), WRITE roles (propose/edit/delete, propose-not-apply), TEST role (dry_run), WRITE/TEST rules (validate/dry_run/attach/detach), PREVIEW (preview_turn), SELECT dropdown sources, and APPLY via existing /api/resolve + /api/apply.

Defines data shapes: RoleFieldSpec, RuleAST (a closed 16-op grammar, no now/random/call/IO op, depth-capped at MAX_RULE_DEPTH=6), RuleDecl, and the 5 destination_kinds (inject, chain, address, surface, lane).
States the governance floor: authoring never writes live state; every write surfaces a proposal carrying the rendered .py source; apply without prior approve is refused HTTP 400 GovernanceError.

Specifies the design bar: gold living-instrument aesthetic (--acc #e6ab5c, warm charcoal base, Fraunces + IBM Plex Mono), status-by-colour, recognition-by-sight, responsive desktop+mobile, reuse kit.tsx/NodeConfigForm/CognitionView.

Lists 9 hard laws (reflects-never-owns, propose-not-apply, closed renderable registry-driven grammar, claude-p/dispatch floor is lead-only with surface-destination kept distinct, run:// addressing, author-from-registry, fail-loud, protected roles, operator-only off the MCP face).
Notes no cognition mockups exist in design/mockups/; CognitionView.tsx is the only living aesthetic reference.

Closes with five open questions for Tim framed as options (one vs two approve gestures, one region vs split, under-specified lane destination, unbuilt directory-scan source, persona theming) plus next-step options.
standouts: The closed, renderable, registry-driven rule grammar (16 ops, 5 destinations, depth cap 6, no IO/now/random op) — determinism is structural, and forbidden destinations (resolve/approve/dispatch) are refused so authoring can never self-dispatch.
Propose-not-apply enforced in the backend: apply without a prior operator approve returns HTTP 400 GovernanceError, proven by use.
The endpoint-count correction with verified-absent /api/cognition/role/apply, showing the brief re-verified the originating task against bridge.py.
preview_turn fires a REAL staged turn (emits to live SSE, writes CAS) without touching chat history — surfaced honestly as intended, not a bug.
conventions: Every claim carries file:line citations and verified-live curl examples; no-fiction notes flag what does not exist.
Frames open questions as options (not binary), consistent with Tim's stated preference, including next-step options at the end.
Author-from-registry / reflects-never-owns / fail-loud / propose-not-apply restated and enforced as laws.
Distinguishes the 'surface' destination kind (an inbox escalation) from the propose-surfaces-for-approval flow to prevent conflation.
notes: The grounded build brief for the cognition authoring screen: roles and rules authored through forms, validated against a closed renderable grammar, surfaced as proposals carrying rendered source, and only made live through the operator approve->apply governance floor.
questions: Should approve and apply be one gesture or two (the brief flags this as genuinely unspecified)?
What is the surface/UX for the under-specified lane destination and the unbuilt directory-scan source node?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is explicitly a build brief for Claude Design and delivers the screen definition, backend contract, UX flow, design bar, laws, and open questions at the grounded, citable level it promises.
apparent_themes: Author cognition through a screen, not by editing files
Governance floor: propose-not-apply, operator approval required
Registry-as-truth (every dropdown derived from an endpoint)
intention_signals: Trying to give Claude Design everything it needs to build the right authoring screen on existing conventions — the exact endpoints, data shapes, reuse components, aesthetic, and the governance laws it must not violate — so the operator can edit the system's thinking visually and safely.
novelty: Authoring a thinking system's roles and rules through a closed, renderable, registry-driven grammar with a hard propose-not-apply floor (and forbidden self-dispatch verbs) is a distinctive design.
contribution: Contributes the cognition authoring screen definition, the verified 12-endpoint backend contract with shapes, the propose->approve->apply UX flow, the gold design bar tied to tokens, and the 9 enforced laws plus open questions for Tim's steer.
summary_for_embedding: A Claude-Design-ready brief for the Company's cognition authoring UI: forms to create/edit roles and rules over a closed 16-op renderable grammar, a 12-endpoint backend contract with file:line citations, a propose->approve->apply governance flow, a gold living-instrument design bar, and nine hard laws.
intention_for_embedding: To specify and ground the cognition authoring screen so it can be built on existing conventions without violating the cognition system's governance floor.
